commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer API/security-boundary acceptance are needed; there is no narrow automated code repair left after the gate fix.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found in the diff; it forwards existing server-side sender context as optional factory params and changes no dependencies, workflows, or secret handling. (sha=eb197b7a2bd6f99c2804e393de62ef5cc840bce6)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds optional trusted sender fields to `ChannelAgentToolFactory` params and forwards `createOpenClawCodingTools` sender context into channel agent-tool factories.

Reproducibility: yes. for the current-main gap by source inspection: channel agent-tool factories receive only `cfg`, while the adjacent OpenClaw tool path receives sender context. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer API/security-boundary acceptance are needed; there is no narrow automated code repair left after the gate fix.

target.title: feat: pass requesterSenderId and senderIsOwner to ChannelAgentToolFactory
target.body:
## Summary

Pass trusted sender context (`requesterSenderId` and `senderIsOwner`) to `ChannelAgentToolFactory`, enabling channel plugins to implement sender-aware access control in their agent tools.

## Problem

`listChannelAgentTools()` only passes `{ cfg }` to channel agent tool factories, while the immediately following `createOpenClawTools()` call in the same function receives the full sender context. This gap means channel-registered agent tools cannot distinguish between different senders.

## Changes

**3 files changed, +17 / -3**

### `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts`
- Extended `ChannelAgentToolFactory` params type to include `requesterSenderId?: string | null` and `senderIsOwner?: boolean`

### `src/agents/channel-tools.ts`
- Updated `listChannelAgentTools` function signature to accept the new fields
- These are already forwarded to the factory via `entry(params)`

### `src/agents/pi-tools.ts`
- Pass `options?.senderId` and `options?.senderIsOwner` from the existing `options` object into `listChannelAgentTools()`

## Backward Compatibility

Fully backward compatible. Existing factories that only destructure `{ cfg }` will simply ignore the new optional fields. No changes required for any existing channel plugin.

## Use Case

The DMWork channel plugin needs sender-aware agent tools where:
- Any user can query their own DM history with the bot
- Any user can query groups they are a member of
- Only the bot owner can query other users' DM history

Without sender context in the factory, the plugin must choose between `ownerOnly: true` (too restrictive) or no access control (too permissive).

Closes #58806

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `ChannelAgentToolFactory` now receives `requesterSenderId` and `senderIsOwner` from the trusted server context, enabling channel plugins to implement sender-aware access control without relying on LLM-controlled input.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `eb197b7a2b`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main (`a002c416c7`), `listChannelAgentTools` is called with only `{ cfg }` — no sender context:

```text
$ grep "listChannelAgentTools" src/agents/pi-tools.ts
921:    ...(includeChannelTools ? listChannelAgentTools({ cfg: options?.config }) : []),
```

Channel plugins have no way to distinguish between different senders.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```text
grep -n "requesterSenderId\|senderIsOwner" src/agents/channel-tools.ts src/agents/pi-tools.ts src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
src/agents/channel-tools.ts:33:  requesterSenderId?: string | null;
src/agents/channel-tools.ts:34:  senderIsOwner?: boolean;
src/agents/channel-tools.ts:101:  requesterSenderId?: string | null;
src/agents/channel-tools.ts:102:  senderIsOwner?: boolean;
src/agents/pi-tools.ts:856:            requesterSenderId: options?.senderId,
src/agents/pi-tools.ts:857:            senderIsOwner: options?.senderIsOwner,
src/agents/pi-tools.ts:924:          requesterSenderId: options?.senderId,
src/agents/pi-tools.ts:925:          senderIsOwner: options?.senderIsOwner,
src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts:  requesterSenderId?: string | null;
src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts:  senderIsOwner?: boolean;

 ✓ unit-fast attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts (20 tests) 5ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  20 passed (20)
   Duration  97ms
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The sender fields are threaded from `createOpenClawCodingTools(options)` through `listChannelAgentTools(params)` into every `ChannelAgentToolFactory` call. The `includeChannelTools` and `includeOpenClawTools` per-family guards are preserved (fixed in `eb197b7a2b`) — no regression to plugin-only/channel-only construction plans. All 20 tool construction plan tests pass.
- **What was not tested**: A live channel plugin consuming `requesterSenderId` for access control was not tested in this proof. The downstream consumer is the [openclaw-channel-octo](https://github.com/nicepkg/openclaw-channel-octo) plugin, which uses these fields to gate cross-user DM history queries. The proof here verifies the core contract (params reach the factory); plugin-side enforcement is tested in the plugin's own test suite.

## P1 Fix (ClawSweeper finding addressed)

The original diff inadvertently replaced `includeChannelTools`/`includeOpenClawTools` guards with `includeCoreTools`. Fixed in `eb197b7a2b`: channel tools remain behind `includeChannelTools`, OpenClaw tools behind `includeOpenClawTools`, while sender context is forwarded in both paths.
target.author: jerry-xin

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
